summary: Compliance delta verified. Internal policies fully synchronized with latest regulatory nodes. Zero drift detected.

changes.old_text: The RBI has released the draft Reserve Bank of India (Trade Receivables Discounting System) Directions with the objective of rationalizing and simplifying the existing regulatory framework.
changes.new_text: Banks shall inform their customer of the receipt of cross-border inward transactions immediately on receipt of inward message.
changes.section_hint: III. Key Provisions of the Draft Directions
changes.affected_section: III. Key Provisions of the Draft Directions
changes.amendment:
**Amended Clause:**

III. Key Provisions of the Draft Directions

*   The NBFC shall inform its customers of the receipt of cross-border inward transactions immediately upon receiving the inward message from the bank, in accordance with the RBI's Trade Receivables Discounting System (TReDS) Directions.
*   This notification shall be made through a secure and reliable means, as specified by the RBI.

trace_reference.circular_section: III. Key Provisions of the Draft Directions
